response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong systems thinking, low-latency DLang experience at Sociomantic, and genuine geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map work. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago with no Rust experience. No demonstrated work on routing pipelines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems either. Worth a screening call given the geospatial domain fit and low-latency distributed-systems chops, but the core language requirement is a significant gap.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput pipelines)
Berlin-based, fluent English, strong open-source and craftsmanship track record
concerns: No modern C++ experience since 2015 and no Rust experience at all
No prior work on routing, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent career trajectory is web/TypeScript/AI tooling, not systems-level pipeline engineering
